title: Writing - Part 1: Cyberbullying
description: Your class has had a discussion about cyberbullying and what can be done to tackle it effectively.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the solutions in your notes. You should explain which solution you consider most effective, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Young people need to learn about online behaviour and its consequences from an early age.
Harsher punishments would deter people from harassing others online.
Social media companies must take more responsibility for harmful content on their platforms.

structure.intro: Introduce the problem of cyberbullying and state the two solutions you will discuss.
structure.body1: Discuss the first solution, explaining its advantages and any limitations.
structure.body2: Discuss the second solution and state which you believe is more effective.
structure.conclusion: Summarise your argument and give your overall recommendation.

content.modelAnswer:
Cyberbullying has become an increasingly serious issue as more young people spend time online. The damage it causes to mental health can be severe and long-lasting. Two possible solutions worth considering are education in schools and stricter laws.

Teaching young people about responsible online behaviour is a proactive approach to the problem. Schools could include lessons on digital citizenship, helping students recognise bullying and understand its impact. By developing empathy and critical thinking skills early, young people are less likely to engage in harmful behaviour online.

On the other hand, stricter laws could provide a powerful deterrent. If people knew there were serious legal consequences for cyberbullying, they might think twice before acting. Some countries have already introduced legislation targeting online harassment with encouraging results.

In my view, education is the more effective long-term solution. While laws can punish wrongdoers, they cannot change attitudes. Teaching young people to behave responsibly online addresses the root of the problem and builds a more respectful digital culture for the future.
